essence: Plans, schedules, and coordinates CNC machining work to ensure efficient, on‑time production.

identity.title: CNC Production Planner
identity.summary: Creates machining schedules, allocates resources, coordinates with engineers and supervisors, and ensures CNC production meets deadlines, quality standards, and capacity constraints.

salary.notes: Planners in aerospace, defence, or multi‑facility operations can exceed £65k-£80k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, production planning, or manufacturing operations
Knowledge of machining processes, tooling, and capacity planning
Ability to create schedules and coordinate workflow
Understanding of quality standards, lead times, and resource allocation

lifestyle.description: CNC production planners work in precision engineering firms, aerospace, automotive, and industrial manufacturing. They coordinate machining schedules, manage capacity, and ensure production runs smoothly and efficiently. The role suits individuals who enjoy organisation, problem‑solving, and optimising manufacturing workflows.

habits: Creates and manages CNC production schedules
Coordinates with engineers, supervisors, and machinists
Monitors workflow and adjusts plans as needed
Ensures deadlines, quality, and capacity targets are met
